title: Providing amounts for the expenses of the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform in the One Hundred Nineteenth Congress.
plain_summary: This bill gives money to a group of lawmakers called the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform. The committee will get about 32 million dollars to pay for their work during the 119th Congress. The money is divided between two different time periods with rules about how much can be spent during each period. The committee must follow special rules when they spend this money that are set by another group called the House Administration Committee.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on House Administration.
subjects: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

sections.title: Section 1  Amounts for Committee Expenses
sections.text: The Committee on Oversight and Government Reform (called the Committee in this resolution) will receive money to pay for its expenses. This includes paying all staff salaries. The money will come from House accounts that are set aside for committee salaries and expenses. The total amount cannot be more than thirty-two million eight hundred sixty-four thousand six hundred thirteen dollars for the One Hundred Nineteenth Congress.

sections.title: Section 2  Session Limitations
sections.text: The money described in Section 1 has limits for different time periods. No more than fifteen million nine hundred seven thousand nine hundred forty-seven dollars can be spent during the first session. This first session starts at noon on January third two thousand twenty-five and ends right before noon on January third two thousand twenty-six. No more than sixteen million nine hundred fifty-six thousand six hundred sixty-six dollars can be spent during the second session. This second session starts at noon on January third two thousand twenty-six and ends right before noon on January third two thousand twenty-seven.

sections.title: Section 3  Vouchers
sections.text: Money under this resolution will be paid using vouchers (official payment requests). The Committee must authorize these vouchers. The Chairman of the Committee must sign them. The Committee on House Administration must approve them in the way they direct.

sections.title: Section 4  Regulations
sections.text: The money made available under this resolution must be spent following the rules set by the Committee on House Administration.
